title: What would Rei be like with a little more personality
display_title: What would Rei be like with a little more personality
wikitext:
[]: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Sun Dec 10, 2017 5:34 pm We all saw in the last episode with the re-imagined world Shinji envisioned, and with Rei with more sunny and cheerful personality, a stark contrast to her nearly emotionless demeanor. And in the Shinji Ikari Raising Project manga, she seems to be one of those shy and timid types, a bit of a kundere, if you will. If she had developed some type personality at some point during the series, even somewhere early on, what kind of girl do you think she would be? Or could have been? And how will affect her interactions with the other characters?

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Sun Dec 10, 2017 6:07 pm I think Rei would be a reserved person, very quiet most of the time, and would say a lot with a few words to people she doesn't know. But, if she is with people she really trusts, her inner personality would come out, and she's be very open to trying new things out, expanding her mind beyond what she knows, and be very friendly and kind. She'd probably be very protective of her friends, almost to the point of self-sacrifice, which isn't much different than what happens, but she would verbalize it better. And she'd know how to stand up for herself as well. Her interactions with Shinji would have been drastically altered, and perhaps she would have played a more active role in fighting and hanging out at the apartment with Shinji, and even being social at school. As for Asuka, she would have put Asuka in her place and defended Shinji from her, and they would have become at least friends.

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Sun Dec 10, 2017 9:04 pm I think I'd put OrdinaryUpbringing!Rei somewhere in between DarkBluePhoenix's post, and EoTV Rei. I personally do not think she would retain much if any kuudere aspects. That's what Rei's known for, and presumably why she's portrayed that way in the Raising Project, but I don't see how she'd turn out that way if she was raised in a more social environment.

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Dec 11, 2017 8:07 pm Rei, while having no agency, definitely always had personality. It's very alien compared to other humans, but it is a personality. To me at least. I think you might find what you're looking for in 2.22, in which Rei's personality is much more proactive.

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by TheCarkolum » Sat Dec 16, 2017 3:14 pm So you're saying: not cheerful personality = no personality at all You should watch Daria.

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sat Dec 16, 2017 5:58 pm I have to highly disagree on your statement that Rei has no personality. In my opinion, she is one of the most well-written characters I have ever seen. So, making her more cheerful as you are implying would only take away from her character and make her way less memorable, on top of not really fitting for both the tone of the show and the circumstances and purpose of her. I mean, which would you remember better after not having seen a show for lets say half a year: A klutzy, hyperactive, maybe kind of slapstick, heroine/possible love interest (the way Rei is in the alternate reality mentioned in the original post) or a mysterious, silent and kind of strange character of the same physical appearance, like Rei is for the rest of the show?

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by Jersey Jimmy » Sun Dec 17, 2017 10:49 am I think DarkBluePhoenix was pretty spot-on. I don't think Rei would ever reach a personality even close to the one in the last episode, but she'd at least express herself more. TheCarkolum wrote: So you're saying: not cheerful personality = no personality at all You should watch Daria.

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Sun Dec 17, 2017 10:56 am Rei with a Personality  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Sun Dec 17, 2017 9:23 pm Correct, DarkBluePhoenix. Also, doesn't she look like a Mii here?

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Sun Dec 17, 2017 10:36 pm imprimatur13 wrote: Correct, DarkBluePhoenix. Also, doesn't she look like a Mii here?

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by Asuka'sBigBrother » Sun Dec 31, 2017 4:01 am A different character.

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by Shinji Ikari Expy » Sun Dec 31, 2017 7:18 pm I hate it when people think being introverted means having "no personality." Still waters run deep. Rei (at least in NGE) is a good example of this.

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Mon Jan 01, 2018 4:40 pm Em she'd be like she was episode 26 when they showed the AU universe where everyone had led a normally life or she'd similar to how she is in the spin off manga's which normally have her as sassy, smart and confident. Though I do feel she still had a personality in the series/manga its just covered by the fear and stress of being replaceable. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Mon Jan 01, 2018 6:21 pm Oh my Lord silvermoonlight that is a hilarious picture.

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Tue Jan 02, 2018 8:52 am imprimatur13 wrote: Oh my Lord silvermoonlight that is a hilarious picture.

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Tue Jan 02, 2018 9:33 am Ah, thanks! You know, you can say what you like about the dub, and its mistranslations, and its lower-quality acting, but it really does have a certain je ne sais quoi that makes it worth watching, despite all that. I actually love when the dubbers modify the source material; when it's done well, you get a viable alternative to the original that can end up giving you two shows for the price of one, or in some cases (such as that of Hetalia), the English version is actually far superior. I say this even though I almost never watch dubs. They still hold a certain charm. (To be fair, that charm is sometimes marveling at the poor acting, but even so, generally worth switching language tracks for an episode or so.)

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Tue Jan 02, 2018 11:45 am imprimatur13 wrote: Ah, thanks! You know, you can say what you like about the dub, and its mistranslations, and its lower-quality acting, but it really does have a certain je ne sais quoi that makes it worth watching, despite all that. I actually love when the dubbers modify the source material; when it's done well, you get a viable alternative to the original that can end up giving you two shows for the price of one, or in some cases (such as that of Hetalia), the English version is actually far superior. I say this even though I almost never watch dubs. They still hold a certain charm. (To be fair, that charm is sometimes marveling at the poor acting, but even so, generally worth switching language tracks for an episode or so.)

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by Blockio » Fri Jan 05, 2018 4:11 pm silvermoonlight wrote: I am just going to leave you this you tube link here to that whole AU scene because that gif does this no justice. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aPyQ6dHsG3I&ab_channel=giblet0

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Sat Jan 06, 2018 1:48 pm Blockio wrote: That scene gets me every time... I actually wouldn't mind if that was made into a full 12-episode series

[]: Re: What would Rei be like with a little more personality?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sat Jan 06, 2018 3:43 pm silvermoonlight wrote: You may want to look in to reading the Shinji Ikari Raising Project manga or playing the Girlfriend/Boyfriend of steel games as both take a slightly more light hearted fan service view of the universe which mirror that episode.
